title: Scope B T6: doctor lint-baseline-filename-format hard error (no --force)
description: Add new doctor lint rule `lint-baseline-filename-format` that detects bare-slug baseline files (filename NOT matching `KT-[A-Z]+-\d+--.*\.md`) in canonical knowledge subdirs and emits a hard error (not `--force` overridable). Resolution message: 'Run `fab scan` to auto-migrate'. Aligns with feedback_cli_design 'drift→abort 不要 --force'.

files.change: Add `createBaselineFilenameFormatCheck(projectRoot: string): DoctorCheck` that scans canonical knowledge subdirs (decisions/, models/, processes/, guidelines/, pitfalls/) under .fabric/knowledge/, lists .md files NOT matching `/^KT-[A-Z]+-\d+--.*\.md$/`. For each violating file, read frontmatter to determine if the id appears baseline-class (KT-MOD/KT-PRO/KT-GLD allowlist). If yes → emit `manual_error` with code `lint-baseline-filename-format`, message lists offending files, resolution 'Run `fab scan` to auto-migrate'. NOT marked as fixable_error (no --fix path; only fab scan migrates). Register in the doctor check registry (createMetaCheck peer position). Personal-layer files outside project root are NOT checked (only project-local canonical dirs).

files.target: new test block for baseline filename lint
files.change: Tests: (1) clean repo (all id-prefixed) → check passes; (2) bare-slug file with baseline-class id in frontmatter → manual_error emitted with file path in message; (3) bare-slug file with non-baseline id (e.g., KP-DEC-0001) → NOT flagged; (4) `--force` flag does NOT silence the error (regression for hard-error contract); (5) resolution message references `fab scan`.

implementation: Add createBaselineFilenameFormatCheck to doctor.ts near createMetaCheck (line ~2225 area)
Wire into doctor's check registry alongside other createXCheck functions
Implement file walk + frontmatter id parse (reuse existing helpers if any; otherwise minimal js-yaml parse)
Verify `--force` does NOT mask the error (assertion in test 4)
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/server test doctor` to confirm green

reference.examples: createMetaCheck shows the DoctorCheck construction pattern; mirror its issueCheck call with severity=error, type=manual_error

criteria: `fab doctor` on a repo with bare-slug baseline file (id in allowlist) emits `lint-baseline-filename-format` manual_error
`fab doctor --force` does NOT silence the error (hard-error semantics preserved)
Resolution message instructs user to run `fab scan`
Non-baseline bare-slug files (e.g., KP-* ids) are NOT flagged
5 new tests pass; existing doctor.test.ts green

rationale.chosen_approach: Hard error + delegated fix path (fab scan) — keeps doctor as inspector, scan as actuator
alternatives_considered: --force escape hatch (违反 cli design 准则)
decision_factors: Precedent: cite_policy rc.20 + drift→abort 准则
Migration path is one-command (fab scan), zero friction
rationale.tradeoffs: Users on legacy layout cannot run doctor cleanly until they run scan once (acceptable; intentional)

manual_checks: Manually create a bare-slug file with baseline id, run fab doctor, observe error
